rationale: NNF is a purely SYNTACTIC restriction on a single wff (def:well-formed-formula): it uses only variables, negated variables (i.e. literals, def:literal), conjunction and disjunction, with every negation immediately before a variable. No logical equivalence appears in the definition, so def:logical-equivalence is not load-bearing and is removed.
notes: Same shape as the def:theory miswiring (batch-0034 0047): a semantic relation attached to a syntactic definition. Logical equivalence properly enters at the NNF CONVERSION procedure (0088) and the EXISTENCE theorem (0089, phi == its NNF), not at the form's definition. Removal, so no licensing span. 'propositional variables, negated propositional variables' = def:literal (could alternatively be def:positive-literal + def:negative-literal); kept as def:literal.
